content.text:
# Design: performance improvement (scope-limited)

## Approach

The research is too thin to commit to a concrete optimization. The single route handler at src/app.ts:5 is the only known surface. I propose first instrumenting it to capture a baseline before changing anything.

## Assumptions

- "Faster" means reducing end-to-end latency of the src/app.ts:5 handler, not throughput, because no metric was specified.
- There is no existing profiling harness, since none was referenced.

## Open Questions

1. What is the target metric and acceptable threshold (p50? p95? cold start)?
2. Where is the measured bottleneck — CPU, I/O, or network? No profile was provided.
3. Are there constraints (memory budget, dependency freeze) that bound the solution space?

I am explicitly NOT inventing a caching layer or rewrite scope until these are answered.
